# jumble

The objective of jumble is to provide a discrete colour palette, as well
as some other useful colours.

## Installation

You can install the development version of jumble from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("davidhodge931/jumble")
```

## Example

jumble provides a discrete palette that is relatively colourblind safe.

``` r
library(jumble)
scales::show_col(jumble)
```

![](reference/figures/README-example-1.png)

``` r
prismatic::check_color_blindness(rev(jumble))
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-2-1.png)

It also provides some other colours with accessible names.

``` r
scales::show_col(
  c(teal, orange, navy, red, pink, grey, slate, blue, ocean)
)
```

![](reference/figures/README-cars-1.png)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`blue`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/blue.md) :
  Blue colour
- [`grey`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/grey.md) :
  grey colour
- [`jumble`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/jumble.md)
  : A jumble of useful colours
- [`navy`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/navy.md) :
  Navy colour
- [`ocean`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/ocean.md) :
  Ocean colour
- [`orange`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/orange.md)
  : Orange colour
- [`pink`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/pink.md) :
  Pink colour
- [`purple`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/purple.md)
  : Purple colour
- [`red`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/red.md) : Red
  colour
- [`slate`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/slate.md) :
  Slate colour
- [`teal`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/teal.md) :
  Teal colour
